entry.desc: Switch to turn on treatment of pre-existing ice in the ice nucleation code. Default: TRUE (CAM6 and CAM7)

Switch to determine whether ice nucleation happens using the incloud (true) or the gridbox average (false) relative humidity. When true, it is assumed that the incloud relative humidity for nucleation is 1. Default: false.

Subgrid scaling factor for relative humidity in ice nucleation code. If it has a value of -1, then indicates that the subgrid scaling factor will be calculated on the fly as 1 / qsatfac (i.e. the saturation scaling factor). Default: 1.2 (MG1/MG2/MG3)

Subgrid scaling factor for relative humidity in ice nucleation code in the stratosphere. If it has a value of -1, then indicates that the subgrid scaling factor will be calculated on the fly as 1 / qsatfac (i.e. the saturation scaling factor). Default: 1.2 (MG1/MG2/MG3)

Fraction of Aitken mode sulfate particles assumed to nucleate ice in the polar stratosphere. Provides an increase in homogeneous freezing over the Liu and Penner method.
Temporary solution to adjust ice surface area density and dehydration in the polar stratosphere where there doesn't seem to be enough nucleation. A value of 1.0 disables the effect. Default: 1.0

Indicates whether to use the tropopause level to determine where to adjust nucleation for the stratosphere (true) or whether to use a hard coded transition level from 100 to 125 hPa applied only in the polar regions (false). Default: true
